query: What is the current state of open-source LLM agent frameworks?
answer: Open-source LLM agent frameworks have started to consolidate around graph-based orchestration as the dominant pattern. LangGraph has pulled ahead in mindshare, with several teams that started on CrewAI's role-based abstraction reporting they migrated to LangGraph once workflows grew branchy or needed durable state. AutoGen remains popular for multi-agent conversation patterns where agents negotiate subtasks with each other. A newly published benchmark leaderboard comparing agent frameworks on long-horizon tool-use tasks shows meaningful gaps in reliability across all frameworks once tasks exceed roughly 15 sequential tool calls. Production deployments are still concentrated in narrow, well-scoped tasks, though enterprise coding-assistant use cases are growing fastest.
